title: Take the name of Jesus with you
firstLine: Take the name of Jesus with you,
publicDomainBasis: Lydia Odell Baxter (1809–1874), William Howard Doane (1832–1915). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Take the name of Jesus with you,
Child of sorrow and of woe;
It will joy and comfort give you,
Take it then where’er you go.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
Take the name of Jesus ever,
As a shield from every snare.
If temptations ’round you gather,
Breathe that holy name in prayer.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
Oh, the precious name of Jesus,
How it thrills our souls with joy;
All the favor of the Father
In this name we may enjoy.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
At the name of Jesus bowing,
Falling prostrate at His feet,
Claim His vict’ry over evil
And the enemy defeat.
